title: hedysarum manaslense var. manaslense
intro: Hedysarum manaslense var. manaslense, also known as Himalayan sweet pea, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant that belongs to the pea family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and powdering the leaves and flowers, which can then be used in teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal purposes.
